highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.139: Agent View & /goal Command (Research Preview)
highlights.summary: Claude Code now includes an 'agent view' accessible via `claude agents`, giving you a unified list of all running, blocked, and completed sessions. The new `/goal` command lets you set a completion condition so Claude keeps working autonomously across turns until the goal is met — great for longer, hands-off tasks.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.142: New Agent Flags & Opus 4.7 in Fast Mode
highlights.summary: The `claude agents` command gains powerful new flags like `--model`, `--effort`, `--mcp-config`, and `--permission-mode` so you can precisely configure background sub-agent sessions. Fast mode has also been upgraded to use Opus 4.7 by default, giving you a better baseline model without any extra configuration.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.143: Plugin Dependency Enforcement & Context Cost Projection
highlights.summary: Disabling a plugin that another enabled plugin depends on will now fail with a helpful copy-pasteable disable-chain command, preventing broken setups. Enabling a plugin also auto-enables its transitive dependencies, and a new projected context cost feature helps you monitor per-turn spending.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has officially announced Claude Opus 4.7, the new default model powering Claude Code's fast mode. This release is important for developers to understand as it directly affects the quality and behavior of Claude Code responses going forward.

highlights.title: How Claude Code Works in Large Codebases
highlights.summary: This Anthropic blog post covers best practices for using Claude Code effectively when your project spans thousands of files — including where to start and how to structure your workflow. If you've found Claude Code struggling with big repos, this guide offers concrete strategies to get better results.

highlights.title: Show HN: adamsreview – Multi-Agent PR Reviews for Claude Code
highlights.summary: adamsreview is a Claude Code plugin that runs multi-stage pull request reviews using parallel sub-agents, validation passes, and persistent JSON state — catching significantly more real bugs than the built-in review. It also supports ensemble review via Codex CLI and can post comments directly to PRs, making it a strong addition to any team's code review workflow.

highlights.title: How Anthropic Engineers Actually Prompt Claude Code (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: This video breaks down the real prompting techniques used by Anthropic's own engineers when working with Claude Code, offering insider insight you won't find in the official docs. Watching how the people who built the tool use it can dramatically improve your own prompting habits and output quality.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: A compact YouTube tutorial that takes you from zero to productive with Claude Code in under 20 minutes, covering both basic and advanced features. This is a great starting point if you're new to Claude Code and want a fast, structured overview before diving into the docs.

tips_of_the_day: Use the new `/goal` command to describe the end state you want (e.g. 'all tests passing with no lint errors') and let Claude Code work autonomously across multiple turns instead of babysitting each step.
Before disabling a plugin, run `claude plugin list` to check for dependents — or just let v2.1.143's new enforcement tell you the exact disable-chain command you need to run safely.

When working in a large codebase, give Claude Code a scoped entry point (e.g. a specific module or feature folder) rather than pointing it at the entire repo — it produces more accurate edits and uses context more efficiently.
